methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Longshot Space is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mike Grace and Nathan Saichek that has raised $10M+. The valuation benchmark universe includes five public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers spanning the sector, including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. This method reflects the capital infusion relative to stage-typical value creation expectations.

Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied in this case; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 45.3x revenue across the six public tickers, Longshot Space's revenue is not on file, precluding any revenue-based multiple application. Peer-median comparison is similarly inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables meeting the threshold of three or more datapoints within relevant filters.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory and path to profitability relative to stage peers, which would refine both public-comp and peer-median approaches if disclosed. The single-method basis reflects data constraints inherent to early-stage private company valuation.
